skill.name: ast-grep-rule-crafter
skill.description: Create ast-grep YAML rules for AST-based code search, lint checks, and rewrite workflows across supported languages.
skill.summary: Craft ast-grep YAML rules for precise code search, linting, and automated refactoring.
skill.author: hylarucoder

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, filesystem, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found Markdown examples and ast-grep syntax rather than executable code. No prompt injection, network access, secret handling, or malicious intent was found. The only residual risk is that users may choose to run documented ast-grep commands that scan or rewrite local project files.

low_findings.title: Documented ast-grep commands can modify files when users run them
low_findings.description: The skill includes Markdown examples for ast-grep scanning and update workflows. These commands are not executed by the skill, but the update mode can rewrite project files when a user deliberately runs it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are user-facing command examples, not executable script code. The low risk is real because one documented command applies file updates when manually executed.

low_findings.title: Markdown examples misclassified as external command execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, and ast-grep pattern syntax. The reviewed files contain documentation only and no script, hook, or runtime command execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are inside Markdown examples and contain YAML or prose. There is no Ruby code, shell backtick execution, or executable file in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: Static weak-cryptography hits are ast-grep metavariable syntax
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives from ast-grep metavariables and pattern examples. Review found no cryptographic operation, hashing implementation, dependency, or secret processing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe ast-grep capture syntax such as metavariables. They do not call cryptographic APIs or recommend weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Filesystem hard-link finding is a Markdown line-number artifact
low_findings.description: The filesystem finding at the reported location is documentation about a Go logging rewrite pattern. Review found no hard-link creation API, filesystem write helper, or executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited line is a Markdown heading for a language example. It contains no filesystem operation and no code that could create a hard link.

content.user_title: Craft ast-grep Rules for Code Refactoring
content.value_statement: Teams need precise code search and refactoring rules without fragile text matching. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through ast-grep YAML patterns, constraints, fixes, and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Explains ast-grep YAML rule structure for search, linting, and rewrite tasks.
Provides examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, and other supported languages.
Shows how to combine pattern, kind, regex, relational, and composite rules.
Guides users through sgconfig.yml setup and project-level rule organization.
Describes fix and transform options for controlled automated rewrites.
Suggests validation workflows using ast-grep scan and debug commands.
limitations: It does not install ast-grep or manage project dependencies.
It does not execute rules or verify matches without a user-provided environment.
Generated rules still need testing against representative source files.
Auto-fix examples may require review before applying changes to large codebases.

use_cases.title: Create Team Lint Rules
use_cases.description: Define ast-grep checks for code conventions that are too structural for regular expressions.

use_cases.title: Plan API Migrations
use_cases.description: Draft search and rewrite rules for replacing deprecated calls with approved alternatives.

use_cases.title: Explore AST Patterns
use_cases.description: Turn a code example into a pattern, add constraints, and debug why matches appear or fail.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Basic Rule
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ast-grep rule that finds a simple function call in my language. Explain the pattern and validation command.

prompt_templates.title: Reduce False Positives
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this ast-grep rule and add constraints or relational rules so it matches only the intended code shape.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Safe Rewrite
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ast-grep rewrite rule for this API migration. Include the fix strategy and a manual review checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Rule Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me organize multiple ast-grep rules for a repository. Include sgconfig.yml guidance, naming conventions, and test cases.

output_examples.input: A developer wants to replace debug logging calls with a project logger.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a named ast-grep rule plan, the match shape, the replacement behavior, and commands for dry-run validation.

output_examples.input: A team needs to prevent bare exception handlers in Python services.
output_examples.output: The skill describes a lint rule using AST node kinds, explains why it avoids text-only matching, and suggests test samples.

output_examples.input: A platform engineer is migrating a deprecated API across TypeScript packages.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines a staged rule design with constraints, transforms, dry runs, and review before applying updates.

best_practices: Start with a narrow pattern, then add constraints after reviewing real matches.
Run scan commands without updates before applying any automated fix.
Keep project rules small, named clearly, and tested against positive and negative examples.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on regex alone when an AST kind or pattern can narrow the match.
Do not apply update commands across a repository before reviewing dry-run results.
Do not use broad metavariables when a specific node kind or constraint is available.

faq.question: What is ast-grep used for?
faq.answer: It searches and rewrites code using syntax trees, which makes matches more precise than plain text search.

faq.question: Can this skill create auto-fix rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains fix and transform patterns, but users should validate results before applying changes.

faq.question: Does the skill run ast-grep commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and command examples. Users decide what to run in their own environment.

faq.question: Which languages are covered?
faq.answer: The references include examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, and many ast-grep supported languages.

faq.question: How should I test a new rule?
faq.answer: Use representative positive and negative samples, run a scan without updates, and inspect matches before adding fixes.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured guidance for creating and reviewing ast-grep rules.
